page.content: In a comparative analysis of age distributions across three countries, the US (n = 127,157), Netherlands (n = 110,944), and Germany (n = 356,520), age group 0–4 years shows values of 0.76 (male), 0.20 (female), and 0.49 (total*) in the US, 0.08 (male), 0.02 (female), and 0.05 (total*) in the Netherlands, and 0.02 (male), 0.01 (female), and 0.02 (total*) in Germany, with ranges of 0.67–0.86, 0.15–0.25, 0.48–0.49, 0.04–0.14, 0.00–0.05, 0.04–0.06, 0.01–0.04, 0.00–0.03, and 0.01–0.02 respectively.
